_note: Culture Tracker — confirmed 2026 World Cup kits, fan moments, merch. Kits released Nov 2025 (Adidas home), Mar 20 2026 (Nike + Adidas away), Mar 24 2026 (Puma).

items.title: Argentina 2026 Home Kit
items.description: The iconic sky-blue and white vertical stripes return with a three-tone fade honouring Argentina's three World Cup wins (1978, 1986, 2022). '1896' stitched on the back neck celebrates the AFA's founding year. Already being called one of the best kits of the tournament.

items.title: Argentina 2026 Away Kit
items.description: Black base with bold swirling blue graphic patterns rooted in Argentina's traditional motifs. The Sol de Mayo sun appears in white accents. A striking alternate to the iconic stripes.

items.title: Netherlands 2026 Home Kit
items.description: The boldest shade of Dutch orange Nike has ever produced. A lenticular federation crest shifts and changes as players move across the pitch — a technical and visual marvel. Orange in Kansas City is going to be spectacular.

items.title: Netherlands 2026 Away Kit
items.description: Historic all-white with a horizontal orange fade running across the kit from chest to hem — symbolising Dutch precision and experimentation. Clean and sharp.

items.title: Belgium 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Bold red jersey inspired by Gothic stained-glass windows. Red Devils and Red Flames icons woven into the design with black and yellow trim. One of Adidas's boldest releases of the cycle.

items.title: Belgium 2026 Away Kit
items.description: Light-blue, pink and white pattern paying direct tribute to René Magritte and the Belgian Surrealist movement. Football meets fine art. The most conversation-starting away kit of the tournament.

items.title: USA 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Nike's co-host statement piece for the USMNT. Clean white with navy and red accents that draw on American heritage and identity. Stars-and-Stripes motifs woven subtly throughout. Designed for a home crowd moment.

items.title: Egypt 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Deep crimson red gradient evoking ancient heritage — angular geometric motifs echo both hieroglyphics and the dynamic pulse of modern Cairo. Black and gold accents complete the design. Salah will wear this at Lumen Field.

items.title: Egypt 2026 Away Kit
items.description: Clean white base with tonal abstract patterns inspired by desert sands, shifting light and timeless Egyptian landscapes. Elegant and understated.

items.title: Australia 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Yellow and green inspired by the beloved 2006 Socceroos kit and the Total 90 era. Gradient green shorts. Pure nostalgia meets modern construction. A crowd-pleaser from Nike.

items.title: Iran 2026 Home Kit
items.description: White body with a subtle tonal animal spot pattern running across both sleeves. The colors of the Iranian flag (green, white, red) trim the sleeve cuffs. The manufacturer logo and chest numbers in red. Understated and elegant.

items.title: Qatar 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Maroon jersey honouring the national flag's famous zig-zag division with darker serrated lines running down the center. Adidas pays homage to Qatar's identity as the 2022 host nation now playing as a visiting team.

items.title: Austria 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Bold red base with deep black sleeves and sharp white trim. Fluid topographic linework maps Austria's mountainous terrain in motion across the fabric. Puma's standout design for Central Europe.

items.title: Ecuador 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Bright yellow base with navy and dark trim — La Tri's vibrant traditional identity, updated for 2026. Clean and classic. One of the more straightforward kits of the cycle but no less effective on the pitch.

items.title: Tunisia 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Strong red base with subtle geometric pattern across the fabric — dark trim on the collar and cuffs adds contrast. Sharp and modern. The Eagles of Carthage in full color at Kansas City.

items.title: Algeria 2026 Home Kit
items.description: White with Algeria's iconic green diagonal sash — a design that traces back decades and remains one of the most distinctive in African football. The Desert Foxes arrive in Kansas City in their classic identity.

items.title: Bosnia-Herzegovina 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Royal blue with yellow diagonal stripe referencing the national flag's star pattern. Bosnia's first World Cup kit in 12 years. Will debut at Lumen Field against Qatar on June 24 in front of Seattle's significant Bosnian diaspora.

items.title: Curaçao 2026 Away Kit
items.description: Soft yellow base with bold pink, turquoise and orange stripes honouring Willemstad's capital and the bright buildings of its Punda and Otrobanda historic districts. One of the most joyful and original kits of the tournament. Adidas celebrates Curaçao's first-ever World Cup appearance.

items.title: Official 2026 World Cup Match Ball — 'Stella'
items.description: Adidas 'Stella' — the official 2026 FIFA World Cup match ball. Thermally bonded, AI-aerodynamics tested. The name references the stellar quality of play the tournament promises. Available in official and replica versions.

items.title: The Azteca hosts its third World Cup
items.description: Estadio Azteca becomes the first stadium in history to host matches at three separate World Cups — 1970, 1986, and now 2026. The opening match (Mexico vs South Africa, June 11) takes place on this sacred ground. Nothing in world football carries more weight.

items.title: Fan sections, tifos, and the art of the terrace
items.description: The American Outlaws (USA), La Doce (Argentina), the Dutch Orange army, and Moroccan and Egyptian ultras are expected to bring some of the most elaborate visual displays ever seen at a World Cup in North America. The culture around the match is as important as the match itself.
